scenario: Apex Capital relies on the Camunda 8.8 Orchestration Cluster REST API to drive 7500 transactions per hour from a Spring Boot integration layer. Before pinning their CI to a new minor release, the platform team needs to confirm the documented stability guarantee that covers the API across upgrades, including how alpha endpoints are treated.
question: Which stability guarantee applies to the Camunda 8.8 Orchestration Cluster REST API?

options.text: The Orchestration Cluster REST API is part of the public API and is covered by SemVer stability guarantees for production use, except for clearly marked alpha endpoints
The Orchestration Cluster REST API is treated as an internal API with no compatibility guarantees, so every minor release may rename or remove endpoints across clusters
The Orchestration Cluster REST API offers backward compatibility only for SaaS deployments, while self-managed clusters must pin to a specific patch version each release
The Orchestration Cluster REST API guarantees backward compatibility across all endpoints including alpha endpoints, which Camunda will keep stable for production traffic forever

a.text: Correct. The overview states this API is part of the Camunda 8 public API and is covered by SemVer stability guarantees for production use, with the explicit exception of clearly marked alpha endpoints.

b.text: Incorrect. The overview promises SemVer backward compatibility for production use, so minor releases must not silently rename or remove stable endpoints.

c.text: Incorrect. The stability guarantee is not scoped to SaaS only; the same SemVer policy covers the API regardless of deployment type.

d.text: Incorrect. The guarantee explicitly excludes clearly marked alpha endpoints, so alpha paths are not covered by the backward-compatibility promise.

explanation: The Orchestration Cluster REST API is part of Camunda 8's public API and is covered by SemVer stability guarantees, so production callers can rely on backward compatibility across upgrades. The only documented exception is endpoints clearly marked as alpha.
